name: Herman Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in half a cup of warm water. Let stand for 5 minutes.
2. In a medium-sized nonmetal bowl, combine the remaining ingredients: sugar, flour, water (110-115°F), and salt.
3. Mix well until combined.
4. Gradually stir in the warm water.
5. Add the yeast mixture and mix thoroughly.
6. Cover loosely and let stand at room temperature for 72 hours, stirring 2-3 times per day.
7. Transfer the fermented mixture to the refrigerator. Stir daily; use within 11 days.
8. To use, take the sourdough starter out of the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for at least 1 hour.
9. Stir well and measure the amount needed for your recipe.
10. Replenish the remaining starter with Herman Food (half a cup sugar, one cup flour, one cup milk) and return to the refrigerator. Use within 2-14 days, stirring daily.
Yield: About 2 cups of starter
Herman Food: Half a cup Sugar, One cup Flour, One cup Milk
